raw:
{
    "items": [
      {
        "prompt": "You are an anatomy exam coach for a patient who just had a car accident and is bleeding heavily from a head injury that may have injured the facial nerve.
Which specific muscle fibers must they consciously control to stop this bleed?

a. Facial muscles of mastication only (from CN V)
b. Facial expression muscles (from VII) or anterior 2/3 taste buds (from VII)
c. Only motor neurons in the trigeminal system

d. Sensory nerves from cranial nerve IX through XII",
        "answer": "Facion facial expressinmss of mastication and anterior two thirds of skin.

The answer carries both motor fibers for muscle contraction (faciation) AND sensory innervation to taste buds, making it the most complete anatomical function tested in this context. The ideal response cites CN VII specifically as being responsible for these functions.",
        "rubric": "- Identify all distinct facial components listed correctly.
- Include sensory input mentioned ('taste').
- Name muscles or structures (faciation) accurately."
      },
      {
        "prompt": "A patient is reporting that when she moves her head side to side, a tingling sensation appears in the area where one of these nerves exits from the skull base.
Which nerve would most likely cause this symptom?

a. CN III ophthalmic (sensory)
b. CN IV tracheal/bronchial (motor only?)
c. CN V trigeminal (motor and sensory via maxillary mandibular divisions).
d. CN VII facial expression.",
        "answer": ""tipping the roof of the nose" is caused by fibers from the maxillary division or mental nerve branches.

The answer carries both motor innervation to muscles involved in chewing/biting ('mastication') AND sensory sensation detected via cheek and ear.'
",
        "rubric": "- Identify the trigeminal root (V) as correct for this symptom description."
      },
      {
        "prompt": "A nurse is preparing a patient who has just undergone a sinusCT scan. A doctor tells them that radiation exposure can damage cranial nerves VII through XI in the skull, while leaving CN V unaffected.

Which nerve remains safe during this procedure?

a. Only facial muscles (CN VII)
b. Just maxilla and mandible movement (CN VIII) or maxillary division of CN V
c. Sensory innervation to skin over nasal cavity and forehead areas involving the two thirds of the face

d. The optic nerve, as it travels with most sensory fibers.",
        "answer": ""The optic nerve is unaffected by this radiation." This applies only because it carries visual data (sensory) through a different pathway than motor fibers to facial muscles.

The answer clarifies that CN V receives no direct radiation in the area covered for 'tipping.' The ideal response identifies specific cranial nerves mentioned or implied regarding safe exposure protocols."
      }
    ]
  },
  "note": null,
  "intervention": null

        *   "A new pattern with 2+ supporting attempts → create it..."

No att_2 light was slow success).
